title: Normalize helper success responses and follow-up semantics
summary: Standardize the success-response contract for workflow helpers so AI agents can determine the resulting canonical state and any remaining lifecycle obligations without relying on implicit behavior. This ticket focuses on helper-style tools such as verify and commit flows, especially the distinction between successful helper completion and terminal lifecycle completion. The key requirement is that helper responses must expose machine-readable canonical guidance, including resulting status, phase, next action, terminality, active transaction identity, and whether follow-up lifecycle completion is still required.
outputs: Standardized success responses for helper-style lifecycle tools
Explicit machine-readable follow-up guidance after verify and commit helper operations
Regression coverage protecting non-terminal committed-state behavior and helper follow-up signaling
acceptance_criteria: Helper success responses expose structured canonical guidance including canonical status, canonical phase, next action, terminal flag, active transaction identity, and follow-up fields.
If a helper succeeds but leaves the transaction in non-terminal committed state, the response explicitly indicates that terminal completion has not yet happened.
Helper responses expose requires_followup and followup_tool consistently when explicit lifecycle closure is still required.
Successful helper completion never implies done unless canonical state is actually terminal.
Agents can determine from helper responses alone whether they must call terminal lifecycle completion next.
Regression tests fail if helper responses regress to ambiguous or incomplete workflow guidance.
tasks: Review current helper success responses in verify and commit flows and identify where workflow guidance is already present versus missing or inconsistent.

Normalize success payloads for helper tools so they expose canonical status, canonical phase, next action, terminality, follow-up requirements, and active transaction identity in a shared machine-readable shape.
Preserve and clarify the existing semantic distinction between non-terminal committed and terminal done.
Ensure helper responses remain truthful to canonical state rather than implying terminal completion prematurely.
Add or update regression tests for helper success responses, especially cases where commit helper success still requires explicit ops_end_task handling.
notes: This ticket is limited to helper-style success responses and their follow-up semantics; it does not cover the full structured failure contract.
The most important behavioral guarantee is that helper completion and terminal lifecycle completion remain distinguishable in machine-readable form.
This ticket should preserve backward compatibility where practical by extending current responses rather than unnecessarily removing existing fields.
